items.title: China’s Collapsing Crude Oil Imports to Fall Further in June
items.summary: The world’s top crude oil importer is set to take even fewer barrels this month, extending its exceptionally subdued appetite since the start of the Iran war.

Hong Kong Dollar Hits 10-Month Low as Fed View Buoys Greenback
items.summary: The Hong Kong dollar fell to its weakest level against the US dollar in about 10 months, as a stronger greenback and expectations for further Federal Reserve rate hikes pressured Asian currencies.

items.title: RT by @MFA_China: ROK President Lee Jae-myung is right to call out Japan’s hollow apologies. Japan’s 35-year (1910-45) occupation of the Korean Peninsula is a living memory. And Tokyo’s pattern of historical amnesia is all too familiar. Many countries that suffered under Japanese invasion and brutality are awaiting a real apology that fully owns the past.
items.summary:
ROK President Lee Jae-myung is right to call out Japan’s hollow apologies. Japan’s 35-year (1910-45) occupation of the Korean Peninsula is a living memory. And Tokyo’s pattern of historical amnesia is all too familiar. Many countries that suffered under Japanese invasion and brutality are awaiting a real apology that fully owns the past.

items.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Inflation Data Eases Rate-Hike Bets
items.summary: Gold steadied near $4,000 an ounce as the latest US inflation gauge tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike, capping a tumultuous week that saw bullion slump to the lowest since November.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
items.summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

items.title: Oil Prices Rise as Ship Struck in Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices turned higher after a ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, sustaining damage to the bridge. Bloomberg's Mica Soellner has the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Sherritt Warns of Going-Concern Risk After Trump Cuba Sanctions

items.title: EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: ‘Do or die’ for small carmakers: China market braces for 156 new models as price war looms
items.summary:
A record 156 new models are expected to hit the Chinese automotive market in the second half of 2026, intensifying concerns about small players’ profitability as a new price war looms.
The mass-market segment would see an influx of intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) priced at around 100,000 yuan (US$14,740), but only leading carmakers were expected to secure large orders thanks to their brand awa

items.title: North Korea’s border build-up opens a Seoul-UN Command rift
items.summary:
A public row between Seoul and the US-led United Nations Command (UNC) has revealed a fresh fault line over how the two interpret North Korea’s recent border fortification activities.
South Korea views its arch-rival’s actions as a violation of the 1953 Korean war armistice agreement. The UNC does not.
The dispute follows a separate disagreement over which authority should govern access to the dem
